entry.title: About Me
entry.content:
Education

Master of Science in Computer Engineering (Software)
Iran University of Science and Technology, Tehran, Iran
2018 - 2021
GPA: 18.69/20.00 (Ranked 1st)
Thesis Title: “Development of a Constraint Solver to Determine the Domain for Complex Data Types” (Grade: 19/20)
Supervisor: Dr. Saeed Parsa
Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering (Software)
Babol Noshirvani University of Technology, Babol, Iran
2013 - 2017
GPA: 17.29/20.00 (Ranked 1st)
Final Project Topic: “Study and Investigation of Routing Protocols in Wireless Sensor Networks” (Grade: 19.5/20)
Supervisor: Dr. Mojtaba Mansouri

Research Interests

Software Testing
Refactoring
Code Generation
Compiler Design
Software Engineering and Object Oriented Design
Design of Programing Languages

Publications

A. Kalaee, S. Parsa, and N. Fathi, “COSMOS: A Comprehensive Framework for Automatically Generating Domain-Oriented Test Suite,” Information and Software Technology, vol. 154, p. 107091, Feb. 2023, doi: 10.1016/j.infsof.2022.107091. (Link)

Technical Skills

Visual Paradigm
IBM Rational Rose
Balsamiq
Qlikview
Microsoft SQL Server
PostgreSQL
Weka
ELKI
ProM tools
C
C#
Java
Python
ASP.NET
IntelliTest
NUnit
Z3 Theorem Prover
CVC4
Coco/R
.NET Compiler Platform (Roslyn)
WordPress
NS-2

Professional Experience

  • Internship
    Analysis of customer relationship management system
    This internship included the preparation of organization chart, task description, context diagram, description of external entities, description of data flow lines, operation diagram, BPMN diagram, use cases diagram, sequence diagram, collaboration diagram, class diagram, and creating wireframe for customer relationship management system. In this regard, IBM Rational Rose and Balsamiq software were used.
    Tavan Tadbir Koosha, Tehran, Iran
    Summer 2016

Academic Projects

  • Analysis of hospital admission system:
    The project included the preparation of organizational chart, task description, context diagram, description of external entities, description of data flow lines, operational diagram, target-scenario model, strategic map, key performance indicators (KPI), management dashboard, BPMN diagram of AS-IS model, BPMN diagram of To-Be model, use case diagram, scenario diagram, sequence diagram, collaboration diagram, and class diagram for the hospital admission system. In this regard, Visual Paradigm and Qlikview software were used.
  • Implementation of the registering and receiving employee information system:
    This C project was done in the Dev-C++ development environment. In this project, it was possible to add a new employee, display the list of employees, search for an employee by name, and delete an employee by employee code. Also, when registering a new employee, it was possible to add the employee's photo and salary slip. All information related to employees was stored in their own files.
  • Implementation of a calculator:
    This Java project was done in the NetBeans development environment. In this project, it was possible to calculate arithmetic expressions including addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, power, remainder, and parentheses. The priority of operators was considered when performing calculations.
  • Implementation a news website:
    In this project, a news website was created using WordPress.
  • Implementation a webservice:
    A SOAP web service was built using the WCF method in the .NET environment. This web service was installed on Windows IIS servers and was used synchronously and asynchronously in the .Net environment. The task of this web service was to convert numbers into letters.
  • Implementation of the warehouse system database:
    For the Database course
    In this project, after drawing the ER diagram of the warehousing system, a database was created in the Microsoft SQL Server database management system, and the relevant tables were added. These tables were filled with data, and several queries were created on them.
  • Implementation of the warehouse system database:
    For the Database Laboratory
    In this project, in the Microsoft SQL Server database management system, several advanced queries were created according to the concepts of procedure, transaction, trigger, and cursor on the already implemented warehousing system database.
  • Implementation of the warehouse system database:
    For the Advanced Database course
    In this project, a warehousing system database was created in the PostgreSQL database management system. The database was connected to Microsoft Visual Studio and a UI was designed for it.
  • Text language detection using n-grams:
    In this project, the language of an input text was recognized using n-grams. In this way, a series of pre-defined words were available in several languages, including Persian and English. The words of the input text were extracted and hashed in order to find out the number of repetitions of each word. 50 words with the highest number of repetitions were compared with pre-defined words in each language, and the number of words that were the same in both were obtained. The language corresponding to the maximum of these numbers was considered as the language of the input text.
  • Extraction of the regression equation:
    In this project, Weka data mining software was used to extract the regression equation of an ARFF file, which was among the ready data sets of the software itself.
  • Spatio-temporal anaylsis of taxis trajectory in Beijing:
    In this project, a dataset containing spatio-temporal information of 10357 taxis in Beijing during one week was given. First, a C# program was written in the Microsoft Visual Studio development environment for random sampling of the data set. Then, by running the DBSCAN clustering algorithm on 6277 random samples in ELKI data mining software, it was determined in which geographic coordinates the density of taxis is higher.
  • Process mining:
    In this project, an event log, which was about the phone repair process in a company, was given, and its BPMN diagram was extracted with the ProM process mining software.
  • Implementation of a multi-layer perceptron classifier with a single hidden layer:
    In this project, a multi-layer perceptron classifier with a single hidden layer for performing both binary and multi-class classification was implemented in Python programing language without using any classes or functions for building the multi-layer perceptron model.
  • Unit testing:
    In this project, after drawing a class diagram in the Visual Paradigm environment, generating C# code from the class diagram, creating a project in the Visual Studio environment, adding classes to the project, and finally implementing the classes, the program methods were unit tested with the help of NUnit tool.
  • Implementation of an automatic code cleanup tool:
    In this project, with the help of the .Net compiler Platform (Roslyn), it was checked whether a C# program is clean according to some principles of clean code.
  • Implementation of a calculator compiler:
    For the Principles of Compiler Design course
    In this project, first, a context-free grammar was written to calculate arithmetic expressions, including addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and parentheses, which was able to consider the priority of operators when performing calculations. Then, with the help of the Lex lexical analyzer generator and the Yacc syntactic analyzer generator, its lexical analyzer and syntactic analyzer were created respectively. Finally, a C header file was created that contained the macro definitions for these two.
  • Implementation of a calculator compiler:
    For the Advanced Compiler course
    In this project, first, a L-attributed EBNF grammar was written to calculate arithmetic expressions including addition, subtraction, multiplication, division and parentheses, which was able to consider the priority of operators when performing calculations. Then, semantic actions were added to it to obtain a feature grammar. Its lexical and syntactic analyzers were created with the help of the Coco/R compiler generator. Finally, a C# main program was created that contained the macro definitions for these two.
  • Resource discovery in grid computing:
    A research was conducted in the field of resource discovery algorithms in grid computing.
  • Load balancing in cloud computing:
    A research was conducted in the field of load balancing algorithms in cloud computing.
  • Routing protocols in wireless sensor networks:
    One of the routing protocols in wireless sensor networks, the flooding protocol, was implemented in the NS-2 simulator.
  • Byzantine fault tolerance:
    A research was conducted in the field of Byzantine fault tolerance methods.

Certifications and Workshops

  • C# Programming-Beginner Certification, LAITEC (belongs to Sharif University of Technology)
  • ASP.NET, LAITEC
  • ASP.NET MVC - .Net Framework, LAITEC

Honors and Awards

  • Ranked 1st among undergraduate students of Computer Engineering, Babol Noshirvani University of Technology, Babol, Iran, 2017
  • Ranked 1st among graduate students of Computer Engineering, Iran University of Science and Technology, Tehran, Iran, 2021
entry.summary:
Education

Master of Science in Computer Engi

entry.title: Master's Thesis
entry.content:
Domain-oriented test suite generation is an essential technique for making software programs relatively more explainable since it can reason about the governing rules changing program behavior through domain boundaries. It is also crucial for detecting latent faults which do not manifest themselves unless the program executes with specific test data from the feasible domain. However, real-world programs typically contain complex data types such as string, struct and array, and black-box called methods that challenge domain coverage. In my master's thesis entitled “Development of a Constraint Solver to Determine the Domain for Complex Data Types”, I transform the domain-oriented test suite generation task into a constraint satisfaction problem. The state-of-the-art constraint solvers generate only one satisfying assignment and support a limited number of data types. Especially they fail when they encounter black-box called methods which considerably affects the performance of the solvers.
To address the challenges, I propose a technique for constraint solving, called DomainSolver, which starts with simplifying the constraint using some testability transformations and splits the result into some sub-constraints each of which contains some expressions with a similar data type. As such splitting guarantees that there is no common variable between the sub-constraints, we can independently solve each sub-constraint by an appropriate strategy. DomainSolver returns the resultant solutions as a set of feasible sub-domains solving the target constraint, provided that it is satisfiable.
I have implemented the proposed approach which uses an underlying compiler as a service, e.g., Roslyn, and assessed the benefits and costs of Domain-Solver with five well-known constraint solvers, e.g, CVC4, S3, Yices2, Z3, and Z3str3. The experimental results, based on two industrial benchmarks with 56 constraints confirm that the approach significantly improves the number of supported data types involved in a constraint, and solves all of the instances on which the other solvers fail while performing in an affordable execution time.
You can access the source code of DomainSolver here.
entry.summary: Domain-oriented test suite generation is an essential technique for making software programs relatively more explainable since it can rea
